articles.headline: Meerut’s historic Sarafa Bazar sees decline post Modi gold appeal
articles.summary: Trading at Meerut's colonial-era bullion market has nearly halted following Prime Minister Modi’s call for citizens to curb gold purchases for a year. The market, which has an annual turnover of ₹20,000 crore, faces significant distress as showrooms offer heavy discounts to attract buyers. Traders have sought relief measures to sustain 30,000 dependent goldsmiths.

articles.headline: Tesla opens fourth Indian experience centre in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Tesla has launched its fourth experience centre in India at Whitefield, Bengaluru. Since entering the Indian market in July 2025, the company has sold over 340 vehicles, primarily from its Model Y lineup. The new facility joins existing centres in Mumbai, Delhi, and Gurugram as Tesla continues to expand its local charging and service infrastructure.

articles.headline: Freshworks introduces no-code Freddy AI Agent Studio for IT management
articles.summary: Software firm Freshworks has expanded its IT service management capabilities by launching Freddy AI Agent Studio. The new no-code platform allows businesses to create domain-specific AI agents or extend existing autonomous agent capabilities. The move is part of the company's broader strategy to integrate agentic AI into enterprise service workflows.

articles.headline: Indian auto sales hit record highs in April despite price hikes
articles.summary: Domestic wholesales for cars, two-wheelers, and three-wheelers saw double-digit growth in April 2026. Passenger vehicle sales reached a record 4.37 lakh units, up 25.4% year-on-year. Two-wheeler sales grew 28.4% to over 18.7 lakh units. The growth occurred despite price hikes of up to 3% by several manufacturers and geopolitical tensions in West Asia.

articles.headline: Tata Motors PV staff to gain ₹168 crore from stock scheme
articles.summary: Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles Ltd has approved a new performance-linked stock incentive scheme that could benefit eligible employees by approximately ₹168 crore. The plan allows staff to acquire up to 50 lakh shares at a face value of ₹2 per share, subject to vesting and performance conditions. The move aims to retain specialized talent in competitive sectors like AI and battery tech.

articles.headline: Tube Investments to invest ₹650 crore in EV and medical units
articles.summary: Tube Investments of India (TII) plans to scale its electric vehicle and medical device businesses with a total capital expenditure of ₹600-650 crore for FY27. The company is seeing strong demand for heavy electric trucks and small commercial vehicles. TII also expects its medical business to grow 15-20% annually following recent facility acquisitions.

articles.headline: DGFT tightens gold import rules for gems and jewellery exporters
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) has restricted duty-free gold imports for jewellery exporters. New guidelines cap import authorizations at 100 kilograms and mandate physical inspections of facilities for first-time applicants. Subsequent licenses will only be granted after exporters fulfill 50% of prior export obligations, aiming to curb the trade deficit.

articles.headline: India confirms safe passage of LPG tankers through Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The government confirmed that two LPG tankers, Symi and NV Sunshine, safely crossed the Strait of Hormuz en route to India. However, an Indian-flagged vessel, Haji Ali, sank in Omani waters after being hit near the shipping corridor; all 14 crew members were rescued. At least 12 Indian vessels remain stranded in the Gulf due to ongoing tensions.

articles.headline: Delhi government orders WFH and vehicle curbs to conserve fuel
articles.summary: In response to a fuel conservation appeal, the Delhi government announced that all its employees will work from home two days a week. Additionally, 50% of official meetings will move online, and petrol allocations for officers will be cut by 20%. The state also introduced a weekly "No Car Day" for citizens and a "Metro Day" for ministers to use public transit.

articles.headline: SEBI proposes simplification of exchange-traded derivatives regulations
articles.summary: Market regulator SEBI has proposed a comprehensive overhaul of derivatives regulations to improve ease of doing business. Key proposals include merging multiple circulars, removing redundant norms, and allowing exchanges to advance expiry dates during sudden market closures. The move aims to simplify compliance for exchanges and clearing corporations.

articles.headline: India mulls tax cuts on bonds to attract foreign investors
articles.summary: The Indian government is considering a significant reduction in taxes paid by foreign investors on national bonds. Recommended by the RBI, the move aims to align Indian policies with global norms and attract capital inflows to fund a rising import bill. The rupee has become Asia's worst performer in 2026, dropping over 6% against the US dollar.

articles.headline: Centre asserts fiscal buffers sufficient to manage global economic shocks
articles.summary: The Finance Ministry stated that India has enough fiscal headroom to manage global turbulence despite a ₹1 lakh crore revenue loss from fuel excise duty cuts. Officials cited prudent management and recent reforms as keys to economic stability. Measures include an Economic Stabilisation Fund and relief windows for SEZ manufacturing units to utilize idle capacity.

articles.headline: Ola Electric in talks to supply battery cells to automakers
articles.summary: Ola Electric is negotiating with global and domestic automakers to supply lithium-ion cells from its Krishnagiri gigafactory. The company intends to expand its manufacturing capacity to 20 GWh by FY28, with one-third earmarked for third-party sales. Ola's wholly-owned subsidiary recently deployed its 4680-format Bharat Cell at scale.

articles.headline: Iran calls on BRICS to condemn US and Israel violations
articles.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Seyyed Abbas Araghchi urged the BRICS grouping to unequivocally condemn violations of international law by the US and Israel. Speaking at the BRICS Foreign Ministers’ meeting in New Delhi, he linked Tehran's actions to the interests of the Global South and described resistance against Western hegemony as a fundamental battle for the group.

articles.headline: Lavrov and Jaishankar discuss trade and sustainable currency logistics
articles.summary: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov and Indian counterpart S Jaishankar held bilateral talks in New Delhi. The ministers discussed developing sustainable financial, transport, and logistics mechanisms to strengthen trade. They also focused on the situation in West Asia and coordinates approaches in multilateral formats like the UN, BRICS, and G20.

articles.headline: US clears Nvidia H200 chip sales to Chinese tech firms
articles.summary: The US Commerce Department has approved sales of Nvidia’s H200 AI chips to 10 Chinese firms, including Alibaba, Tencent, and ByteDance. The move comes as CEO Jensen Huang seeks to unlock stalled deliveries in China. While the H200 is Nvidia's second-most powerful chip, no deliveries have occurred yet due to ongoing US-China tech tensions and export curbs.

articles.headline: TN CM urges removal of cotton import duty amid crisis
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay has written to Prime Minister Modi requesting the removal of the 11% import duty on cotton. He cited a severe industry crisis caused by a 25% spike in cotton prices and yarn shortages. Removing the duty would ensure raw material availability for garment manufacturers and help them meet export commitments.

articles.headline: HAL reports 6% rise in Q4 net profit to ₹4,196 crore
articles.summary: Hindustan Aeronautics Ltd (HAL) posted a consolidated net profit of ₹4,196 crore for the fourth quarter of FY26, a 6% increase year-on-year. Revenue grew 1.7% to ₹13,942.4 crore. The company’s profitability was bolstered by a 76.6% jump in other income. For the full fiscal year, HAL's consolidated profit rose 9% to ₹9,115.52 crore.

articles.headline: ED freezes ₹526 crore in Gameskraft money laundering case
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate has frozen bank deposits worth ₹526 crore and seized gold jewellery valued at ₹3.5 crore after searches at online gaming platform Gameskraft. The raids, conducted in Delhi-NCR and Bengaluru, were part of a money laundering investigation. The agency also seized ₹11 lakh in cash during the operation.

articles.headline: Pesticide makers seek balanced approach following Paraquat herbicide ban
articles.summary: CropLife India has called for a balanced policy after the government proposed a nationwide ban on Paraquat Dichloride due to health risks. The industry body wants the decision to be vetted through rigorous scientific processes and suggests tighter usage protocols instead of a total ban. The herbicide market for the chemical is estimated at ₹1,500 crore annually.

articles.headline: Water storage in India’s major reservoirs drops below 35%
articles.summary: Storage levels in India’s 166 major reservoirs have fallen to 34.45% of total capacity, with the southern region showing the lowest levels at 25%. Data from the Central Water Commission indicates that storage is currently 13 percentage points higher than last year but remains critical in several states like Telangana and Kerala due to deficient rainfall.

articles.headline: Amul and Mother Dairy hike milk prices by ₹2 per litre
articles.summary: Major dairy brands Amul and Mother Dairy have announced a liquid milk price hike of ₹2 per litre across India and the NCR, respectively. The increase, roughly 2.5-3.5%, follows a year of stable prices. Both companies cited rising operational and procurement costs, including cattle feed and fuel, as reasons for the revision.

articles.headline: Quick commerce becomes key online growth engine for FMCG brands
articles.summary: FMCG giants like Britannia, Nestlé, and Dabur are seeing up to 80% of their e-commerce business coming from quick-commerce platforms. With over 50 million monthly users, rapid delivery services are influencing daily purchase behaviors and helping companies premiumize their portfolios. The channel is expected to hold a 15-20% share of the packaged food market by 2030.

articles.headline: Supreme Court reserves order on Sabarimala women discrimination petitions
articles.summary: A nine-judge Constitution Bench of the Supreme Court has reserved its order on petitions challenging discrimination against women at religious places, including the Sabarimala temple. The bench heard arguments for 16 days regarding the scope of religious freedom and the conflict between individual rights and religious practices across multiple faiths.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh allots land to Reliance for massive AI data centre
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh Cabinet has approved the allotment of 855 acres in Visakhapatnam to Reliance Industries for a giga-scale AI data centre. The project, involving an investment of ₹1.08 lakh crore, is expected to generate over 1,000 jobs. The state also cleared several other investments, including a Royal Enfield manufacturing facility in Tirupati.

articles.headline: Delhi CM announces WFH and Metro Mondays to conserve fuel
articles.summary: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta launched the 'Mera Bharat, Mera Yogdan' campaign, mandating two-day work-from-home for government offices and 'Metro Mondays' for officials. The measures include a 20% cut in official fuel quotas and a one-year ban on official foreign visits. Private firms are encouraged to adopt similar fuel-saving drills following PM Modi's appeal.

articles.headline: India's wholesale inflation surges to 8.3 percent in April
articles.summary: Wholesale Price Index (WPI) inflation hit 8.3% in April, driven by an oil shock and a weak rupee. This 4.4 percentage point jump from March is the biggest consecutive monthly increase in the current series. The rise is attributed to global crude oil prices, which surged by 67.2% annually, and currency depreciation. Analysts had predicted a much lower print.

articles.headline: Delhi HC judge initiates contempt against Arvind Kejriwal
articles.summary: Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma initiated contempt proceedings against former Delhi CM Arvind Kejriwal and four other AAP leaders for social media posts targeting her and her family. She withdrew from hearing the CBI's appeal in the excise policy case, noting that a judge who initiates contempt cannot continue the underlying matter. The case will be assigned to a new bench.

articles.headline: Iran and UAE clash at Brics meeting in Delhi
articles.summary: The first day of the Brics foreign ministers' meeting in New Delhi saw sharp exchanges between Iran and the UAE over the West Asia conflict. Iran's Seyed Abbas Araghchi accused the UAE of allowing its territory to be used for US strikes against Iran. India expressed concerns over disruptions to energy infrastructure and the security of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Storms and lightning kill 111 in Uttar Pradesh
articles.summary: At least 111 people died and 72 were injured as violent hailstorms, heavy rain, and lightning struck 25 districts of Uttar Pradesh. Wind speeds reached up to 130 kmph in Bareilly and Prayagraj. Most fatalities were caused by collapsing walls, falling trees, and lightning strikes. CM Yogi Adityanath directed officials to accelerate relief operations and provide compensation.

articles.headline: Two arrested for gang-rape on private bus in Delhi
articles.summary: A 30-year-old woman was allegedly gang-raped by two bus drivers inside a private vehicle in outer Delhi's Rani Bagh area. The accused, Umesh and Ramendra Kumar, were arrested and sent to judicial custody. The bus had tinted windows and curtains in violation of Supreme Court norms. Police confirmed the assault occurred while the vehicle was parked near Nangloi.

articles.headline: SC acknowledges executive veto in election commission appointments
articles.summary: The Supreme Court flagged the 'executive veto' in appointing top poll officers, noting that the selection panel's 2:1 majority effectively gives the government control. The bench questioned why a Union minister replaced the Chief Justice of India in the panel. The court emphasized that an independent Election Commission is vital for democracy and free elections.

articles.headline: DU objects to Susan Elias's appointment as Stephen's principal
articles.summary: The University of Delhi has directed St Stephen’s College to halt the appointment of Professor Susan Elias as principal, alleging the selection committee violated UGC regulations. DU claimed it was not asked to nominate experts for the panel. Elias was slated to be the college's first woman principal. The university warned the college not to proceed until norms are met.

articles.headline: 38 civilians held hostage in Manipur after tribal killings
articles.summary: Manipur Home Minister Govindas Konthoujam confirmed that 38 people from the Naga and Kuki communities are being held hostage by various groups. The situation follows an ambush that killed three church leaders. The state is engaging with civil society to secure their release. Normal life remains paralysed in several districts due to shutdowns called by tribal organizations.

articles.headline: Honda registers first annual loss in 70 years
articles.summary: Japanese automaker Honda reported a 423.9 billion yen ($2.7 billion) loss for the 2025-26 fiscal year, its first since listing in 1957. The company cited heavy costs associated with electric vehicle plans and shifts in US trade policies. Vehicle sales dropped to 3.4 million units. However, the company forecast a return to profit for the next fiscal year.

articles.headline: ED to attach Iqbal Mirchi's assets in Dubai and Mumbai
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate is set to attach properties worth hundreds of crores linked to deceased narcotics trafficker Iqbal Mirchi and his family. The action covers land in Mumbai’s Worli and real estate in Dubai under the Fugitive Economic Offenders Act. A special court previously declared Mirchi's wife and sons as fugitive economic offenders.

articles.headline: Man arrested in Kolkata for killing newlywed wife for dowry
articles.summary: Delhi Police arrested a man in Kolkata for allegedly strangling his 28-year-old wife over dowry demands in central Delhi's Nabi Karim. The victim's body was found in a bed-box on May 8. The accused confessed to the murder, stating he was upset that his wife's family could not afford the cash and electronics he demanded. He had fled to Kolkata after the incident.

articles.headline: Woman chartered accountant jumps to death in Gurugram
articles.summary: A 32-year-old woman chartered accountant allegedly jumped to her death from the fifth floor of a Gurugram mall. Her husband was arrested for abetment to suicide after the victim's family alleged mental torture and financial control. The victim worked for an insurance firm and had reportedly complained of depression. Police have also booked her in-laws in the case.

articles.headline: Teacher arrested for aiding sexual assault of 3-year-old girl
articles.summary: Delhi Police arrested a woman teacher for allegedly helping a caretaker sexually assault a three-year-old girl in a private school basement in Janakpuri. The teacher is accused of luring the child with toffees and thrashing her to keep her quiet. The main accused had been arrested earlier on May 1. The victim's family accused the police of delaying the teacher's arrest.

articles.headline: Delhi launches hydrogen-powered shuttle service for Central Vista
articles.summary: The DMRC has operationalised a two-bus hydrogen-powered shuttle service connecting Central Vista government offices to Metro stations. The first-of-its-kind initiative in Delhi aims to improve last-mile connectivity and promote public transport. The project is a collaboration with Indian Oil and various ministries. Buses feature GPS tracking and CCTV.

articles.headline: Protests at Jantar Mantar over NSA charges on Noida workers
articles.summary: Human rights activists and professors gathered at Jantar Mantar to protest the invocation of the National Security Act (NSA) against two individuals in Noida. The UP Police accused them of inciting factory workers to violence. Protesters called the charges baseless and demanded the release of several activists and workers they claim were wrongly implicated.

articles.headline: NCRTC offers branding rights for 21 Delhi-Meerut stations
articles.summary: The National Capital Region Transport Corporation (NCRTC) is offering semi-naming and co-branding rights for 21 stations on the Delhi-Meerut Namo Bharat corridor. The move aims to generate non-fare revenue. Bidders can apply to prefix or suffix brand names to station identities, including major hubs like Anand Vihar and Sarai Kale Khan.

articles.headline: Delhi sanctions 28.8 crore rupees to clean Yamuna drains
articles.summary: The Delhi government has approved ₹28.8 crore for in-situ biological and chemical treatment of three major drains—ISBT, Sen Nursing Home, and Jaitpur—that empty into the Yamuna. The project aims to reduce the pollution load in the river's most contaminated 22km stretch. Work is expected to begin next month and conclude within five months.

articles.headline: Revenue department takes over PM-UDAY scheme from DDA
articles.summary: The Delhi government has transferred the implementation of the PM-UDAY scheme, which grants property rights in unauthorised colonies, from the DDA to the revenue department. Dedicated cells will be established in all 13 districts, with Additional District Magistrates designated as the competent authorities to process applications and execute conveyance deeds.

articles.headline: Vande Mataram made mandatory in West Bengal schools
articles.summary: The BJP-led government in West Bengal has mandated the singing of all six stanzas of 'Vande Mataram' during morning assemblies in all state-run and state-aided schools. The order follows a Union home ministry directive. The move has sparked debate, as previous administrations only used the first two stanzas, citing secular considerations.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu government announces 2 percent DA hike
articles.summary: Chief Minister Joseph Vijay announced a 2% hike in Dearness Allowance (DA) for 1.6 million Tamil Nadu government employees and teachers, raising it to 60%. The state also announced the transfer of several senior IAS officers in a major reshuffle. Additionally, the government confirmed that ₹1,000 monthly entitlements for women will be credited soon.

articles.headline: AIADMK factions seek disqualification of rival MLAs in Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: Rival factions of the AIADMK have petitioned the Tamil Nadu Assembly Speaker to disqualify each other's legislators following a split over supporting the ruling TVK government. One faction led by Edappadi Palaniswami and another by C Ve Shanmugham are battling for party control. The split became evident during a recent floor test where the two groups voted differently.

articles.headline: SC bench questions essential religious practices doctrine in Sabarimala
articles.summary: A nine-judge Supreme Court bench reserved its verdict in the Sabarimala reference case, questioning the 'essential religious practices' (ERP) doctrine. The court observed that the test could be 'elitist' by privileging certain traditions over others. The case examines the interplay between religious freedom and equality following the 2018 verdict allowing women's entry.

articles.headline: India monitors Sri Lanka's offer for strategic Hambantota airport
articles.summary: India is closely watching Sri Lanka's move to offer the Mattala Rajapaksa International Airport near Hambantota to foreign investors. The airport, built with Chinese loans, is strategically located near a Chinese-controlled port. New Delhi is concerned about regional security and maritime flows, especially after a previous joint venture involving Indian and Russian firms failed.

articles.headline: Outgoing CDS Chauhan links theaterisation delay to Operation Sindoor
articles.summary: Chief of Defence Staff General Anil Chauhan stated that the creation of unified theatre commands was delayed by recent operational commitments like Operation Sindoor. He emphasized that military reforms are essential for survival. The next CDS, Lt Gen NS Raja Subramani, will be responsible for fast-tracking the integration of the Army, Navy, and Air Force under single commanders.

articles.headline: HC recalls Siddharth Varadarajan’s OCI card relief for suppression
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court recalled its order granting OCI card conversion to journalist Siddharth Varadarajan after finding he suppressed a court order barring him from foreign travel without permission. Justice Kaurav termed the non-disclosure 'serious.' Varadarajan's counsel apologized for the omission. The court will now decide if further action should be initiated.

articles.headline: SC summons Rajasthan and MP officials over sand mining
articles.summary: The Supreme Court summoned top officials from Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh over their failure to stop illegal sand mining in the National Chambal Gharial Sanctuary. The court expressed shock at 'administrative lethargy' and the free movement of vehicles used by the mining mafia. Officials have been ordered to appear personally on May 20 to explain the lack of enforcement.

articles.headline: Shivraj Singh Chouhan releases book on PM Modi's digital vision
articles.summary: Union Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan's new book, 'Apnapan', explores Prime Minister Narendra Modi's early efforts to digitize the BJP. The book recounts anecdotes from the late 1990s when Modi introduced email to party workers. It also covers major events like the 1991 Ekta Yatra and the resolution of the Narmada water dispute, highlighting Modi's long-term strategic thinking.

articles.headline: Lakhimpur Kheri student dies by suicide over NEET-UG cancellation
articles.summary: A 20-year-old medical aspirant allegedly died by suicide in Lakhimpur Kheri, Uttar Pradesh, following the cancellation of the NEET-UG 2026 exam. His family claimed he was under severe mental stress after his third attempt was voided due to the paper leak. No suicide note was found. The incident highlights the emotional toll of the exam's compromise on over 2 million students.

articles.headline: NTA’s 448 crore surplus questioned amid security failures
articles.summary: The National Testing Agency (NTA) accumulated a surplus of ₹448.21 crore from exam fees over five years, despite security lapses leading to the NEET-UG cancellation. A parliamentary panel had previously recommended using these funds to strengthen vendor monitoring and in-house testing capacity. Aspirants criticized the agency for failing to secure exams despite high fee collections.

articles.headline: Kolkata police officer arrested in multi-crore land grab case
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate arrested Kolkata DCP Shantanu Sinha Biswas for his alleged involvement in multi-crore land transactions and extortion. The arrest followed the capture of alleged land grabber Joy S Kamdar. ED officials claim Biswas refused to reveal the source of huge monetary transactions found on his phones. The officer had previously served in CM Mamata Banerjee's security.

articles.headline: Charges framed against MP Amritpal Singh in Ajnala attack
articles.summary: An Amritsar court framed charges of attempt to murder and criminal conspiracy against Khadoor Sahib MP Amritpal Singh for the 2023 Ajnala police station attack. The radical preacher and his supporters had stormed the station to free an associate. Amritpal appeared via video link from Dibrugarh jail. Charges under the Arms Act were dropped due to lack of weapon recovery.

articles.headline: India condemns drone attack on cargo ship off Oman
articles.summary: India denounced an attack on the Indian-owned cargo vessel Haji Ali in Omani waters, calling the targeting of commercial shipping 'unacceptable.' All 14 Indian crew members were rescued by the Oman Coast Guard after the ship caught fire and sank. The attack is believed to involve a drone or missile, occurring amid heightened tensions in the region involving the US and Iran.

articles.headline: Pakistan test-fires Fatah-4 long-range cruise missile
articles.summary: Pakistan successfully test-fired the indigenously developed Fatah-4 ground-launched cruise missile. The military stated the weapon is capable of engaging long-range targets with high precision. The launch was intended to validate technical parameters and improve operational efficiency. This follows previous tests of the Fatah-2 system as Pakistan upgrades its Rocket Force Command.

articles.headline: India’s March crude imports fall 17 percent amid Iran war
articles.summary: India’s crude oil imports declined 17.1% in March 2026 compared to the previous year due to the US-Iran conflict and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Despite higher global prices, India’s total import bill fell by 5% as volumes dropped. PM Modi has urged citizens to reduce fuel consumption to conserve foreign exchange as the rupee hit a record low against the dollar.

articles.headline: Jannik Sinner breaks Djokovic's Masters 1000 winning record
articles.summary: World No. 1 Jannik Sinner achieved a record 32nd consecutive win at ATP Masters 1000 events, surpassing Novak Djokovic's 2011 tally of 31. Sinner beat Andrey Rublev 6-2, 6-4 in the Rome quarterfinals to secure the milestone. The 24-year-old Italian has dominated the circuit, winning five straight Masters titles and dropping only two sets during his historic run.

articles.headline: Nikhat Zareen crashes out of CWG and Asiad trials
articles.summary: Two-time world champion Nikhat Zareen lost 1-4 to Sakshi Chaudhary in the flyweight semi-finals of the national selection trials. The defeat means Nikhat will miss both the Commonwealth Games and the Asian Games. It marks a significant setback for the 29-year-old boxer, who has struggled to find form since the Paris Olympics. Sakshi will now face Minakshi Hooda in the final.

articles.headline: Virat Kohli hits unbeaten century to lead RCB victory
articles.summary: Virat Kohli scored his ninth IPL century, hitting an unbeaten 105 to propel Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a victory over KKR. The innings made Kohli the first Indian to score 10 T20 hundreds and the fastest player to reach 14,000 T20 runs. His disciplined chase cemented his reputation as the 'chase master,' using a risk-free approach to secure vital points for his team.

articles.headline: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi named in India A squad for tri-series
articles.summary: 13-year-old batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has been included in the 15-member India A squad for an upcoming tri-series in Sri Lanka. The team, led by Tilak Varma, will face A-sides from Sri Lanka and Afghanistan. Sooryavanshi’s inclusion is seen as a step toward his fast-tracking into the senior national team following his record-breaking performances in the U-19 World Cup.

articles.headline: Madonna, Shakira and BTS to perform at World Cup final
articles.summary: FIFA announced that Madonna, Shakira, and BTS will headline the first-ever halftime show during the 2026 World Cup Final at MetLife Stadium. The Super Bowl-style concert is part of a partnership with Global Citizen. The event aims to raise $100 million for global education. This marks the first time a FIFA final will feature a major mid-game musical performance.

articles.headline: Diljit Dosanjh revealed as US citizen on American passport
articles.summary: Reports have revealed that actor-singer Diljit Dosanjh has held US citizenship and travelled on an American passport since 2022. The news comes amid political pressure in Punjab, where CM Bhagwant Mann alleged Diljit is being targeted. Dosanjh previously dismissed rumours of joining politics. His wife, Sandeep Kaur, is also a US citizen, according to the report.

articles.headline: Indian couple swims 32km from Sri Lanka to India
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based IT professionals Vrushali Prasade and Danish Abdi became the first Indian couple to swim across the 32km Palk Strait. They completed the journey from Sri Lanka to India in 11 hours, overcoming challenges like jellyfish stings and salt-induced swelling. The couple, who only learned to swim four years ago, are now planning to cross the English Channel.

articles.headline: Calbee Japan switches to monochrome packaging over ink shortage
articles.summary: Japanese snack giant Calbee has announced it will switch the packaging of its flagship products to a simple black-and-white design. The radical change is due to a shortage of ink reserves, aiming to avoid a total production halt. While some see it as an eco-friendly move, the company stated it was a necessary measure to ensure snack availability during the supply crisis.

articles.headline: PM Modi Urges Austerity Measures to Stabilize National Economy
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has called on citizens to adopt austerity measures for one year to ease pressure on foreign exchange reserves. Recommendations include avoiding non-essential gold purchases, overseas holidays, and destination weddings. The appeal comes as the West Asia war disrupts supply chains and increases the national import bill for crude, edible oils, and fertilizers.

articles.headline: Cabinet Approves Higher MSP for 14 Kharif Crops for 2026-27
articles.summary: The Union cabinet raised the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for 14 kharif crops to bolster food security and reduce import reliance. Sunflower seeds saw the highest hike of ₹622 per quintal, followed by cotton and pulses. The total procurement outlay is estimated at ₹2.6 trillion, with the government focusing on incentivizing oilseeds and pulses to curb food inflation.

articles.headline: India Reports 0.67 Million Disaster-Linked Internal Displacements in 2025
articles.summary: A report by the Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre ranks India among the top 10 countries affected by disaster-linked internal displacements in 2025. While South Asian displacements fell due to a milder monsoon, extreme weather events and conflict-linked tensions in Manipur and near the Line of Control resulted in hundreds of thousands of people being uprooted from their homes.

articles.headline: Petroleum Ministry Confirms 60-Day Strategic Crude Oil and Gas Stock
articles.summary: Petroleum Minister Hardeep Singh Puri announced that India currently holds 60 days of available crude oil and natural gas stocks. Despite the ongoing West Asia war, the minister assured that there is currently no supply problem for the country's energy requirements.

articles.headline: Government Raises Gold and Silver Import Duty to 15%
articles.summary: The Centre has increased the import duty on gold and silver to 15% from the previous 6%. The decision aims to curb precious metal imports, ease pressure on foreign exchange reserves, and narrow the trade deficit as the rupee faces external market pressures.

articles.headline: NTPC Considers ₹56,000 Crore Nuclear Power Investment in Bihar
articles.summary: State-run NTPC Ltd is planning to invest ₹56,000 crore to establish 2.8 GW of nuclear power generation capacity in Bihar. The project will be executed through its subsidiary, NTPC Parmanu Urja Nigam Ltd, as part of the company's expansion into clean energy infrastructure.

articles.headline: Heavy Industries Ministry Clears Funding for 4,874 Public EV Chargers
articles.summary: The heavy industries ministry has cleared ₹503.86 crore for the installation of 4,874 public electric vehicle (EV) charging stations. This initiative is part of the PM E-Drive scheme, which targets a total installation of 72,000 EV chargers to support the country's transition to electric mobility.

articles.headline: Trump and Xi Meet in Beijing Amid West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping met to discuss a diplomatic breakthrough in the West Asia conflict. Both leaders agreed that the Strait of Hormuz must remain open for global energy flow. However, global oil markets remain on edge following fresh attacks on commercial vessels in the region and continued disruptions to nearly one-fifth of global oil supplies.

articles.headline: Air India Reports Estimated $3 Billion Loss for Fiscal Year 2026
articles.summary: Tata Group-owned Air India posted an estimated loss of nearly $3 billion in FY26. The losses were driven by high jet fuel prices, airspace disruptions following the closure of Pakistani airspace, and significant foreign exchange losses. Despite the losses, the airline's revenue reached approximately ₹79,200 crore for the fiscal year.

articles.headline: PSU Banks See Sharp Drop in Non-Core Treasury Income
articles.summary: A Mint analysis of 36 listed banks shows that rising government bond yields hit treasury income in the March quarter. Public sector banks reported a 22% drop in other income, the steepest in 15 quarters. In contrast, private banks weathered the quarter better due to more diversified income streams including wealth management and retail fees.

articles.headline: RBI Relaxes Overseas Remittance Rules for Banks and Non-Bank Partners
articles.summary: The RBI has dispensed with the requirement for banks to seek prior approval for tie-ups with non-bank entities for outward remittances. Banks are now responsible for ensuring regulatory compliance by their partners. The move aims to streamline cross-border payments for non-trade purposes like education and medical treatment.

articles.headline: DGFT Imposes 100kg Cap on Gold Imports Under Advance Authorization
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Foreign Trade has introduced a 100kg limit on gold imports under the Advance Authorization scheme. The tighter norms, which include mandatory physical verification of facilities, aim to prevent misuse of the duty-free scheme for price arbitrage following the hike in gold import tariffs to 15%.

articles.headline: Government Plans to Regulate Private Doctor Consultation and Surgery Fees
articles.summary: The Centre is considering amending the Clinical Establishment Act to mandate range-bound consultation fees and standard rates for common medical procedures at private hospitals. The move aims to reduce out-of-pocket expenses for patients, as private hospitalization costs reached an average of ₹50,508 in 2025. The plan faces expected pushback from the medical community.

articles.headline: GSFC to Produce Automotive Urea to Resolve Supply Shortages
articles.summary: The Centre plans to rope in Gujarat State Fertilizers and Chemicals (GSFC) to manufacture technical-grade urea used in diesel exhaust fluid (Adblue). Currently, India faces a 450,000-tonne supply gap, relying heavily on imports. Bolstering domestic production is seen as critical for the diesel vehicle industry amid West Asia supply chain disruptions.

articles.headline: India Urges Brics Solidarity Over West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: Foreign Minister S. Jaishankar urged Brics members to work toward a stable international order and called for specific attention to the West Asia war. India is attempting to bridge divisions within the bloc as the conflict continues to impact global energy security and trade.

articles.headline: Dhruva Space Receives ₹105 Crore Grant for Project Garud
articles.summary: Hyderabad-based startup Dhruva Space has been awarded ₹105 crore from the Centre’s Research, Development and Innovation Fund. The grant will support Project Garud, which focuses on developing a standardized 500 kg-class satellite platform for high-volume deployment.

articles.headline: AI Adoption Leads IT Firms Toward Outcome-Based Pricing Models
articles.summary: Top IT services firms like Infosys, Cognizant, and HCL Tech are shifting from traditional hourly billing to fixed-price or outcome-based contracts. This transition is driven by AI automation, which reduces the manual hours required for software tasks. Fixed-price contracts currently account for roughly 54% of Infosys's revenue.

articles.headline: ASK Alternates Launches ₹2,500 Crore Second Private Credit Fund
articles.summary: ASK Alternates has launched its second private credit fund with a target corpus of ₹2,500 crore. The fund will target a 14-16% gross return by investing in market-leading businesses backed by reputed promoters or private equity. The focus sectors include infrastructure, healthcare, manufacturing, and renewables.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Stays Key Proposals in Kapur Family Legal Battle
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has stayed the appointment of additional directors and changes to bank signatories for Raghuvanshi Investment Pvt. Ltd. The order comes amid an ongoing dispute within the Kapur family over the RK Family Trust. The court referred the matter to mediation under former CJI D.Y. Chandrachud while urging an amicable settlement.

articles.headline: Cement Firms Brace for Margin Squeeze Amid Rising Fuel Costs
articles.summary: Indian cement makers are facing rising fuel, freight, and packaging costs due to the West Asia conflict. Despite efficiency measures like using domestic coal and FGD gypsum, companies like Dalmia Bharat and UltraTech reported pressure on profits. Analysts suggest price hikes may not be enough to offset the structurally higher energy costs.

articles.headline: Bajaj Group to Invest ₹2,000 Crore in Healthcare Expansion
articles.summary: The Bajaj group plans to diversify into the healthcare sector with a ₹2,000 crore investment in Pune over the next three years. Under the Bajaj Integrated Health System, the group aims to expand into 12 cities over the next decade, focusing on medical intervention and preventive care.

articles.headline: Mahindra Exits Spanish Firm CIE Automotive in Non-Core Divestment
articles.summary: Mahindra and Mahindra Ltd (M&M) has sold its remaining 3.58% stake in CIE Automotive SA for €126 million. The exit is part of M&M's strategy to shed loss-making or non-core international subsidiaries to focus on its domestic automobile, tractor, and aerospace businesses.

articles.headline: Panda Express to Enter India Market in 2027
articles.summary: US-based Asian fast-casual chain Panda Express plans to enter India in 2027 through a partnership with Trimex Foods. The brand aims to target the ₹300-500 price segment in urban markets, tapping into growing aspirational demand for international Chinese dining concepts.

articles.headline: Sebi Proposes Overhaul of Commodity Derivatives Trading Framework
articles.summary: Market regulator Sebi has proposed removing the 'close-to-the-money' (CTM) category in commodity options contracts. The move aims to simplify trading procedures and reduce compliance burdens for exchanges. Most global commodity exchanges do not use the CTM framework, which Sebi says creates uncertainty for sellers.

articles.headline: Mitsui OSK Lines Targets Indian Energy and Automobile Shipping
articles.summary: Japanese shipping giant Mitsui O.S.K. Lines (MOL) plans to expand its presence in India's energy transportation and automobile export sectors. The company, which operates the world's second-largest fleet, also intends to increase its recruitment of Indian seafarers, who already make up 20% of its global workforce.

articles.headline: Amazon Market Value Nears $3 Trillion on AI Optimism
articles.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is approaching a $3 trillion market capitalization as investors cheer its AI strategy. The stock has gained 36% since late March, driven by strong growth in Amazon Web Services and demand for its custom AI chips. Amazon recently reported over $225 billion in revenue commitments for its cloud and AI services.

articles.headline: Kevin Warsh Confirmed as Fed Chair in Record Narrow Vote
articles.summary: The US Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as Chairman of the Federal Reserve with a 54-45 vote, the narrowest margin ever for the position. Warsh succeeds Jerome Powell during a period of high political pressure on the central bank. Only one Democrat supported Warsh, reflecting fears that he may compromise the Fed's independence.

articles.headline: Domestic Passenger Vehicle Dispatches Jump 25.4% in April
articles.summary: India's passenger vehicle dispatches rose to 437,312 units in April 2026, marking a strong start to the fiscal year. Utility vehicles were the primary growth driver with a 21.5% increase. Despite high commodity prices, the industry continues to see robust demand, with two-wheeler sales also rising by 28.4% last month.

articles.headline: Dubai Real Estate Market Cools Following Regional Geopolitical Shocks
articles.summary: Dubai's property market is experiencing a slowdown after a four-year boom, with transaction volumes dropping 24% between January and March 2026. While prices remain steady, investors are becoming more selective amid the West Asia conflict. However, underlying demand for villas and high-end units remains supported by the Golden Visa programme.

articles.headline: Intel-AMD Veterans' Agrani Labs Seeks $100m for AI Chips
articles.summary: Agrani Labs, a startup by former Intel/AMD executives, seeks $100 million Series A to build AI inference chips compatible with Nvidia's CUDA software stack, aiming for a $400-500 million valuation.

articles.headline: D2C Brands Face Pinch as Consumer Spends Drop Amid Inflation
articles.summary: Direct-to-consumer brands are squeezed by rising input costs and falling discretionary spending as consumers cut back, with potential for 5-6% drop in spending over three months.

articles.headline: Mid-tier IT Firms Outpace Giants in Revenue Growth
articles.summary: Mid-tier IT services firms like Coforge and Persistent Systems are outpacing larger rivals, posting 7-28% YoY revenue growth, attributed to their focused, adaptable strategies.

articles.headline: Shadowfax Reports Rs 56 Cr Q4 Profit, Sales Up 74%
articles.summary: New-age logistics firm Shadowfax reported a net profit of Rs 56 crore in Q4FY26, with operating revenue up 74% YoY to Rs 1,237 crore, driven by strong order delivery.

articles.headline: Electric 2-Wheeler Cos Ramping Up Output on Demand Surge
articles.summary: Leading EV two-wheeler makers are aggressively expanding capacity to meet booming demand, fueled by rising petrol prices and consumer acceptance, projecting over 150,000 units monthly output.

articles.headline: India Inc Biggies Turbocharge Plans to Move Logistics into Electric Lane
articles.summary: Top companies are electrifying fleets to cut fossil fuel use, with PepsiCo, Maruti Suzuki, and Hindustan Unilever adopting EVs and improving rail logistics, boosting demand for electric commercial vehicles.

articles.headline: Too Few Tech Roles for Overflowing Talent Pool
articles.summary: India's IT sector faces a talent surplus, with active jobseekers up 32% to 650,000 in April. Mid-level roles are saturated, while demand for specialized skills like AI/ML remains high.

articles.headline: Oil Stocks Down 15% Since Onset of Iran Conflict
articles.summary: India's crude oil stocks have fallen 15% since the Iran conflict began, as refiners draw down inventories, potentially forcing them to scale back operations if supply constraints persist.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Corners Centre Over ECI Independence
articles.summary: The Supreme Court questioned the government's 'show of independence' in appointing election commissioners, suggesting the LoP's role is ornamental and querying the exclusion of the CJI from the selection panel.

articles.headline: CBI Probing Public Servants’ Possible Role in NEET Paper Leak
articles.summary: CBI is investigating potential involvement of public servants in the NEET-UG paper leak, arresting two more accused, and probing access to the printing press where the exam paper was published.

articles.headline: BRICS FMs Push For Dialogue in West Asia, Free Hormuz Passage
articles.summary: BRICS Foreign Ministers urged dialogue for peace in West Asia, stressed unimpeded maritime flows, and advocated for a two-state solution for Palestine amid geopolitical tensions.

articles.headline: K'taka, Chile Eye Tech Ties for Digital Services and Startups
articles.summary: Karnataka plans to strengthen collaboration with Chile in digital services, startups, and talent exchange, building on previous agreements to foster innovation and market access.

articles.headline: Congress Leadership Picks Satheesan as Kerala CM
articles.summary: The Congress high command named V.D. Satheesan, the outgoing leader of opposition, as the new Kerala chief minister, marking a significant feat despite lacking prior ministerial experience.

articles.headline: Wholesale Inflation Touches 3.5-Year High of 8.3% in April
articles.summary: India's wholesale inflation surged to an 8.3% high in April, driven by rising crude oil, gas, metals, and minerals prices, sparking fears of imminent retail fuel price hikes.

articles.headline: US Farmers Dial Crop Adjusters as Wild Weather Hammers Wheat
articles.summary: US wheat farmers are facing severe crop damage from extreme weather, including hail and unseasonal warmth, leading them to contact crop insurance adjusters as harvest approaches.

articles.headline: Tech Stocks Power Wall Street Gains as Cisco Soars
articles.summary: Wall Street advanced, led by tech stocks and chipmakers, with Cisco Systems shares surging 15% on a solid outlook, as renewed AI wagers and economic resilience fueled market momentum.

articles.headline: D-Street Surges 1% on Reports Govt to Cut Tax on Foreign Bond Bets
articles.summary: Indian equity indices rose over 1% as reports suggested a government tax cut on foreign bond investments, boosting sentiment and rupee recovery amid market volatility.

articles.headline: Rupee Almost Breaches 96/Dollar, Rebounds Amid Intervention
articles.summary: The Indian rupee hit an intraday low of 95.96/$ on Thursday before recovering slightly, with analysts expecting further weakening due to forex outflows and inflation.

articles.headline: JSW Steel Posts 13x Rise in Profit for Q4 on One-Time Gain
articles.summary: JSW Steel reported a nearly 13-fold rise in consolidated net profit for Q4FY26, aided by a one-time gain from BPSL Steel's slump sale, and guided for production capacity of 78 MT by FY32.

articles.headline: Faltering CPSEs to Face Strict Penalties for Lapses
articles.summary: The Centre has tightened performance norms for CPSEs, imposing penalties for CSR lapses, delayed MSME payments, and lack of succession plans, aligning with 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision.

articles.headline: BoB Looks to Sell Rs 2.7k Cr of NPAs; Taps ARCs, NBFCs
articles.summary: Bank of Baroda plans to sell Rs 2,776 crore of NPAs to ARCs and NBFCs, inviting bids for 41 accounts, including nine declared fraud-tagged, as it cleans its balance sheet.

articles.headline: Kaynes' Q4 Show Has Analysts Downgrading After Revenue Miss
articles.summary: Kaynes Technology shares plunged 20% after a weaker-than-expected Q4 earnings performance and revenue guidance miss, with analysts citing execution delays and cash burn.

articles.headline: Adani Enters Hospitality Biz with IHG Hotel Pact
articles.summary: Adani Airport Holdings and IHG Hotels & Resorts will develop five hotels with 1,500 rooms in four Indian cities, marking Adani Group's formal entry into the hospitality sector.

articles.headline: China's Credit Growth Slips Below Estimates Amid Weak Demand
articles.summary: China's credit expansion slowed significantly in April, with households repaying loans, indicating subdued demand and raising concerns about the country's economic recovery.

articles.headline: Pricier Oil Adds to Beijing's EV Push as Gasoline Demand Slides
articles.summary: China's gasoline demand is projected to slide further this year due to rising oil prices and a long-term shift to EVs, potentially contracting by 5.5% amid overcapacity in refining.

articles.headline: WPI Hits 3.5-Year High of 8.3% in April Amid War Impact
articles.summary: India's wholesale inflation reached an 8.3% high in April, driven by global commodity price hikes due to the West Asia war, raising risks of cascading effects on retail inflation.

articles.headline: Sugar Exports Banned till Sept 30 to Stabilize Domestic Prices
articles.summary: India has banned sugar exports until September 30 to stabilize domestic prices, citing lower-than-expected production and potential El Niño impact, though it may affect mill owners' liquidity.

articles.headline: Six Major IT Cos’ Share Value Erodes by $186 Billion
articles.summary: Six major Indian IT companies have seen their market capitalization fall by $186 billion (45%) from their peak in December 2024, reflecting the impact of AI disruption.

articles.headline: Karnataka HC Sets Aside KIADB Order Against Embassy Arm
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court quashed the KIADB's order to resume 78 acres of land held by Embassy East Business Park, allowing the company to continue its proposed business park development.

articles.headline: Jio Platforms Appoints Akash Ambani as Managing Director
articles.summary: Jio Platforms has appointed Akash Ambani as its managing director ahead of its IPO, marking a significant leadership transition for the digital services company.

articles.headline: India Wants Higher Audit Walls, Global Firms Uneasy
articles.summary: Proposed changes to India's auditor independence rules, including a three-year cooling-off period, are causing unease among global professional services networks due to potential impact on business models.

articles.headline: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Praises China's Tech Ambitions
articles.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang praised China's tech ambitions, aligning with Elon Musk's vision, as part of the US business delegation's visit amid geopolitical tensions and trade disputes.

articles.headline: US Retail Sales Growth Slows on Month Amid Higher Gas Prices
articles.summary: US retail sales rose 0.5% in April, a slowdown from March, as higher gas prices and fading tax refund benefits impacted consumer spending on non-essentials.

articles.headline: United Spirits Logs 3% Rise in Sales in Q4 Despite Maharashtra Policy
articles.summary: United Spirits reported 3.7% sales growth in Q4FY26, despite Maharashtra's liquor policy hurting cheaper brands, with net sales rising to Rs 3,054 crore.

articles.headline: Jaguar Land Rover Profit Wiped Out by Cyberattack, US Tariffs
articles.summary: Jaguar Land Rover's profit was almost erased last year by a cyberattack, US tariffs, and demand slump in China, with pre-tax profit falling to £14 million.

articles.headline: Genomics, Next-gen Sequencing to Define Diagnostics for Next Decade
articles.summary: Metropolis Healthcare plans to leverage genomics, next-gen sequencing, and strategic acquisitions for growth, despite intensifying competition, aiming to expand services beyond pathology.

articles.headline: Kansas City Prepares for World Cup as Soccer Hotspot
articles.summary: Kansas City, a World Cup host city, is preparing for teams like Argentina, England, and Netherlands to base themselves there, leveraging its central location and growing soccer infrastructure.

articles.headline: AI Takes Molière to Stage in France with New Play
articles.summary: A play written by AI in the style of Molière, 'The Astrologer, or False Omens,' premiered at the Royal Opera of Versailles, showcasing AI's potential in cultural creation.

articles.headline: Mysterious World Beyond Pluto May Have an Atmosphere
articles.summary: Japanese astronomers detected an atmosphere around a minor planet beyond Pluto, potentially formed by erupting ice volcanoes or comet impacts, challenging previous understanding.

articles.headline: Ray-Ban Heir Files Legal Challenge to 10-Billion-Euro Family Fortune Deal
articles.summary: Rocco Basilico, heir to Ray-Ban, is challenging a $10 billion deal that could reshape control of EssilorLuxottica, alleging improper voting thresholds and payouts.

articles.headline: Python: UK Football Clubs Face Spying Allegations
articles.summary: Southampton faces allegations of spying on rivals ahead of a crucial playoff match, highlighting a history of such incidents in football and the EFL's move towards tougher rules.

articles.headline: One Way of Tracking the Apocalypse? Follow Billionaires' Jets
articles.summary: A coder has created an 'apocalypse early warning system' by tracking private jet movements, suggesting that a large number of jets fleeing a city could signal an emergency.

articles.headline: France's Macron Croons Ballads at State Dinner in Armenia
articles.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron sang classic ballads, including 'La Bohème,' at a state dinner in Yerevan, accompanied by the Armenian Prime Minister on drums.

articles.headline: PSG Seal 5th Straight Ligue 1 Title, Beat Palace 3-0
articles.summary: Paris Saint-Germain secured their fifth consecutive Ligue 1 title by beating Crystal Palace 3-0, maintaining pressure on Arsenal in the title race.

articles.headline: Manchester City See Off Palace to Keep Pressure on Arsenal
articles.summary: Manchester City's second string beat Crystal Palace 3-0 to climb two points behind Arsenal with two games left in the Premier League title race.

articles.headline: CBI probes potential insider role in NEET-UG 2026 paper leak case
articles.summary: The CBI has intensified its investigation into the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak, focusing on an 'insider role' within the examination system. One suspect was arrested from Pune and a medical professor was detained in Latur. Probes are currently active across multiple states including Rajasthan, Bihar, and Maharashtra, following allegations of organized cheating and paper distribution.

articles.headline: 117 dead as storms lash Uttar Pradesh with cyclone-like winds
articles.summary: Violent thunderstorms with winds reaching 130kmph have claimed 117 lives across Uttar Pradesh. The storms caused widespread destruction of homes, uprooted trees, and led to significant crop loss, particularly in Amroha and Bijnor. The state government has announced a compensation of Rs 4 lakh each for the families of the deceased as relief operations continue across affected districts.

articles.headline: Centre bans sugar exports to boost domestic availability and control prices
articles.summary: The Union government has banned the export of raw, white, and refined sugar until September 30 to ensure sufficient domestic supply and stabilize prices. The move comes as export levels reached 7.3% higher than previous seasons, potentially depleting local stocks. This restrictive measure is aimed at curbing food inflation and prioritizing the domestic consumer market.

articles.headline: Xi Jinping warns Trump that Taiwan row may lead to conflict
articles.summary: China's President Xi Jinping has issued a stern warning to US President Donald Trump, stating that disagreements over Taiwan could lead to a 'very dangerous' conflict. The statement follows recent US moves that China views as provocative. Both leaders expressed shared interest in reopening the Strait of Hormuz, but tensions remain high over sovereignty and trade issues.

articles.headline: Lucknow HC orders CBI and ED probe into Rahul Gandhi's assets
articles.summary: The Lucknow bench of the Allahabad High Court has directed the CBI and ED to investigate the alleged disproportionate assets of Congress MP and Leader of Opposition, Rahul Gandhi. The court has requested a progress report by July 20. The probe follows a public interest litigation questioning the funding for Gandhi's foreign trips and personal wealth accumulation.

articles.headline: Lucky escape for 10 babies in Nagpur hospital fire incident
articles.summary: Ten newborn babies were safely evacuated after a smoke-filled fire broke out in the neonatal ward of Daga Memorial Govt Women's Hospital in Nagpur. The fire, which started on Wednesday, triggered panic among staff and parents. Sources allege hospital administration failed to inform senior health authorities about the incident for nearly 24 hours. No injuries were reported.

articles.headline: Forger's markcard sparks 40-year identity theft and pension battle
articles.summary: A 40-year-old case of identity theft has surfaced after a Bengaluru man used a forged markcard to secure a government job. The impostor, who worked for the Karnataka Electricity Board, allegedly paid hush money for decades to the original student whose identity he stole. A dispute over pension payments led the victim to file a police complaint, leading to the impostor's arrest.

articles.headline: BMTC to enable advance QR ticket bookings for bus commuters
articles.summary: The Bangalore Metropolitan Transport Corporation (BMTC) is set to allow passengers to book QR-based digital tickets via mobile apps before boarding. This move aims to reduce reliance on cash and speed up the boarding process. Additionally, BMTC plans to distribute 11,000 new Electronic Ticketing Machines (ETMs) and start accepting NCMC 'One City One Card' payments from June.

articles.headline: Pulmonary blood clots affect 1 in 1000 people; surgical awareness low
articles.summary: Experts warn that pulmonary thromboendarterectomy (PTE) is a life-saving but underutilized surgery for patients suffering from chronic pulmonary blood clots. While pulmonary embolism affects 1 in 1,000 individuals, many remain unaware of advanced surgical options that can significantly improve outcomes. Early diagnosis and specialized medical intervention are critical to preventing heart failure.

articles.headline: 35 poor quality drugs detected at government Jan Aushadhi Kendras
articles.summary: A special drug drive across 230 government-run Jan Aushadhi Kendras found 35 drug samples to be of 'not-of-standard quality' (NSQ). Health Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao confirmed that the samples, including commonly used medicines, were failed by the FDA. Authorities are now prosecuting the two companies responsible for manufacturing these substandard generic drugs to ensure public safety.

articles.headline: Karnataka Congress plans door-to-door voter roll revision to prevent exclusions
articles.summary: The Karnataka Congress is preparing for a massive month-long door-to-door voter roll verification starting June 30. The party aims to cover 5.5 crore voters to ensure no genuine citizens, particularly those from minority and underprivileged sections, are excluded from the list. This follows concerns that nearly 51 crore voters were deleted in previous phases across several Indian states.

articles.headline: Digital surveillance at KSR railway station to recognize habitual offenders
articles.summary: KSR Bengaluru Railway Station has commissioned a high-tech surveillance system featuring facial recognition and video analytics. The system can match faces against a database of known offenders and alert security personnel in real-time. With over 2.5 lakh daily passengers, the technology aims to curb habitual theft and enhance safety across the station's entry points and platforms.

articles.headline: Proposed building violation rules in Bengaluru trigger debate on regularization
articles.summary: The Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA) has received 25 objections regarding proposed changes to building violation rules. The new bylaws could allow for the regularization of building violations up to 15% through compounding fees. Critics argue this sends the wrong signal by repeatedly legitimizing illegal constructions, while officials state it could help regularize minor deviations.

articles.headline: Centre tells SC: Gender equality does not apply to religious rights
articles.summary: The Union government has informed a nine-judge Supreme Court bench that gender equality as a constitutional mandate does not apply to religious rights. Solicitor General Tushar Mehta argued that the right to practice religion includes the right to follow traditional rituals and beliefs, even if they prescribe different modes of worship for men and women, without interference.

articles.headline: EAM Jaishankar highlights fragile maritime traffic amid West Asia tensions
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar emphasized the fragility of global maritime trade due to ongoing tensions in the Red Sea and West Asia. He called for a rules-based international order to protect vital shipping routes and ensure the safety of seafarers. Jaishankar reiterated India's commitment to de-escalation and its support for a two-state solution to the Israel-Palestine conflict.

articles.headline: Manipur guns ignite fresh flashpoint after three years of conflict
articles.summary: New violence has erupted in Manipur's eastern hill districts, with village volunteers in Kamjong district targeted by Myanmar-based militant groups. Local authorities reported that militants used high-end weapons and tactical gear in the attacks. The conflict, which began in early 2023, has seen a resurgence despite increased presence of central security forces and various peace initiatives.

articles.headline: Delhi mandates two days of work-from-home to curb pollution levels
articles.summary: The Delhi government has ordered all state departments and private offices to implement work-from-home for two days a week. The move is part of measures to reduce vehicular pollution and ease traffic congestion in the national capital. Residents are also encouraged to use public transport and voluntarily observe 'no vehicle days' to help improve the city's deteriorating air quality.

articles.headline: PBKS beat MI as Shardul Thakur's 4/39 secures close victory
articles.summary: Punjab Kings (PBKS) secured a thrilling victory over Mumbai Indians (MI) in their IPL match at Dharamsala. PBKS set a target of 201 before Shardul Thakur's exceptional bowling figures of 4/39 helped restrict MI's chase. Despite a valiant knock from MI's Tilak Varma, PBKS held their nerve to win by 6 runs, moving up the points table and keeping their playoff hopes alive.

articles.headline: Russia pummels Kyiv with missile strikes; 10 dead as peace hopes dented
articles.summary: Russian forces launched a series of missile and drone attacks on Kyiv and other major cities, killing at least 10 people and wounding dozens more. Ukrainian air defenses intercepted most of the 675 missiles and drones fired, but several reached their targets, damaging energy infrastructure and residential buildings. The attacks have cast a shadow over recent diplomatic efforts for a ceasefire.

articles.headline: Supreme Court warns current CEC selection process favors government choice
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has expressed concern that the current process for appointing Election Commissioners (ECs) allows the central government to select candidates of its choice. A bench of Justices suggested that a more independent panel, potentially including the CJI and LoP, would ensure greater neutrality. The court highlighted the need for a transparent system to uphold the poll body's integrity.

articles.headline: ED raids online gaming firms in Bengaluru; assets worth Rs 527cr seized
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted raids at multiple locations in Bengaluru and other cities targeting online gaming companies. Assets worth Rs 527 crore, including bank balances and luxury properties, were seized as part of an investigation into illegal betting and money laundering. Authorities are probing foreign exchange violations and the use of shell companies to move funds abroad.

articles.headline: VD Satheesan Named Kerala Chief Minister After Leadership Stalemate
articles.summary: VD Satheesan, a six-time MLA, has been elected leader of the Congress legislative party in Kerala, ending a ten-day suspense. He emerged as the preferred choice over contenders like KC Venugopal and Ramesh Chennithala, with support from allies. Satheesan is credited with rebuilding the party, and his swearing-in is expected May 18.

articles.headline: Inox Clean Energy Acquires US Solar Assets for $750 Million
articles.summary: Inox Clean Energy has acquired US solar manufacturing assets from China's Boway Group for $750 million. The deal includes an operational 3 GW module facility and a 3 GW cell facility under construction, marking the company's entry into the US market and aiming to leverage domestic manufacturing incentives.

articles.headline: Xi Jinping Warns Trump of Taiwan Conflict; Opens Doors to US CEOs
articles.summary: During a Beijing summit, Xi Jinping warned Donald Trump that mishandling Taiwan could lead to conflict, while also telling US CEOs like Musk and Cook that China's doors will "open wider." Discussions covered trade divisions and cooperation, with deep differences on Taiwan and a US arms package noted.

articles.headline: India's WPI Inflation Surges to 42-Month High of 8.3% in April
articles.summary: Wholesale price-based inflation in India rose to 8.3% in April, driven by a sharp spike in fuel and power prices (24.71%). Crude petroleum surged 88.06%. This rise, linked to the West Asia crisis, fuels fears of sustained retail price pressure.

articles.headline: India Bans Sugar Exports Until September 30 to Stabilize Prices
articles.summary: The Indian government has imposed an immediate ban on sugar exports through September 30 to ensure domestic supply and curb inflation. This move aims to retain roughly 0.2 million tonnes of sugar domestically. Exemptions exist for specific EU and US quotas.

articles.headline: Tata Motors Q4 Profit Drops 32% Amid JLR Weakness
articles.summary: Tata Motors' consolidated net profit fell 31.7% to ₹5,783 crore in Q4FY26, dragged by Jaguar Land Rover's slowdown despite record volumes in India's passenger vehicle business. Consolidated revenue rose 7.2%.

articles.headline: Sunil Mittal Plans Leadership Transition, Aims for Majority Stake in Airtel
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel Chairman Sunil Bharti Mittal plans to hand over leadership within 10 years and regain a promoter stake over 50% in the company. He also aims to increase Airtel's stake in its African subsidiary to 90%.

articles.headline: JSW Steel Profit Surges on Record Sales, Driven by Bhushan Power Transfer
articles.summary: JSW Steel reported a Q4 net profit surge to ₹16,370 crore, boosted by record steel sales and an exceptional gain from transferring its Bhushan Power & Steel business to a JV. Normalized profit was ₹3,475 crore.

articles.headline: UK Health Secretary Wes Streeting Resigns to Challenge Starmer's Leadership
articles.summary: UK Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigned, calling for a leadership contest to replace PM Keir Starmer, citing the Labour Party's 'drift' after poor local election results. He's the first senior minister to challenge Starmer.

articles.headline: Kaynes Technology Shares Crash 20% After Q4 Profit Miss
articles.summary: Kaynes Technology shares plunged 20% after reporting a 21.5% YoY decline in Q4 net profit to ₹91.2 crore, attributed to rising costs. Brokerages downgraded the stock due to concerns over working capital and missed revenue guidance.

articles.headline: Rupee Hits Record Low of 95.96 Against US Dollar Amid Tensions
articles.summary: The Indian rupee reached a lifetime low of 95.96 per US dollar, pressured by high crude oil prices and capital outflows. It has weakened over 6% since the West Asia conflict began, becoming Asia's worst-performing currency of 2026.

articles.headline: Uber to Establish Tech Centres in Bengaluru and Hyderabad by 2027
articles.summary: Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi announced the company will set up major technology centres in Bengaluru and Hyderabad by end-2027. The Bengaluru centre will house 5,000 employees, underscoring India's importance for Uber's engineering and its two-wheeler mobility segment.

articles.headline: US Authorities Move to Resolve Fraud Cases Against Gautam Adani
articles.summary: US prosecutors are reportedly moving to resolve and potentially drop fraud charges against Gautam Adani as early as this week. A parallel SEC civil case may also be settled with a penalty, impacting the conglomerate's market standing.

articles.headline: Roche Pharma Launches Subcutaneous Immunotherapy for Lung Cancer
articles.summary: Roche Pharma has introduced Tecentriq SC in India, a subcutaneous immunotherapy for lung cancer that takes only seven minutes to administer, aiming to improve patient experience and reduce indirect costs.

articles.headline: CBI Arrests Two More in NEET UG Paper Leak, Links to NTA Insider
articles.summary: The CBI arrested two more individuals in the NEET (UG) paper leak case and informed a court that the leak originated from an NTA insider. Searches were conducted at 14 locations across India.

articles.headline: EC Announces Third Phase of Electoral Roll Revision for 16 States
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India is launching the third phase of the Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls in 16 states and 3 UTs, including Delhi, to ensure accuracy in voter inclusion.

articles.headline: NIA Charges 10 in Red Fort Blast, Links to Al-Qaeda Affiliate
articles.summary: The NIA charged 10 individuals, including doctors, for plotting 'Operation Heavenly Hind' by al-Qaeda-linked Ansar Ghazwat-ul-Hind to overthrow the government. The Red Fort car bomb blast was part of this plan.

articles.headline: Air India Group Losses Exceed $2 Billion in Fiscal 2025-26
articles.summary: Air India Group reported a $2.80 billion loss for FY25-26, impacted by West Asia conflict and airspace bans, setbacking Tata Group's turnaround plans. Singapore Airlines noted its share of these losses.

articles.headline: Karnataka CM Bans Saffron Shawls in Schools, Allows Existing Religious Symbols
articles.summary: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah clarified that saffron shawls are not permitted in educational institutions, though existing religious symbols like turbans are allowed, even as the hijab ban is being withdrawn.

articles.headline: FMCG Firms Hike Prices Amid Rising Commodity Inflation
articles.summary: FMCG companies like HUL and Britannia are raising prices or reducing pack sizes due to increased costs for fuel, packaging, and raw materials like edible oil. Commodity prices rose nearly 10% in March.

articles.headline: Singapore Airlines Profit Falls 57% Driven by Air India's Losses
articles.summary: Singapore Airlines reported a 57.4% drop in net profit, largely due to its share of Air India’s significant losses. Air India faced headwinds from airspace restrictions and rising fuel costs.

articles.headline: India Requests US Extension on Russian Oil Waiver Amid Iran War
articles.summary: India has asked the US to extend its waiver on Russian oil purchases, prioritizing supply security amid the ongoing Iran war and volatile energy markets which could impact 1.4 billion citizens.

articles.headline: Honda Motor Reports First Annual Loss in Nearly 70 Years on EV Costs
articles.summary: Honda Motor posted its first annual loss since 1957, hit by over $9 billion in EV restructuring costs and scrapping EV targets, highlighting risks in electric vehicle investments.

articles.headline: Global Investors Eye $1.3 Billion Adani Airport Investment
articles.summary: A consortium including Temasek and Alpha Wave is negotiating a $1.3 billion investment in Adani Group's airports business, potentially valuing it at $18 billion amidst rising air traffic in India.

articles.headline: Oracle Pulls Campus Offers, Rattling IITs and NITs
articles.summary: Oracle has revoked dozens of job offers to students at IITs and NITs due to 'internal restructuring,' causing anxiety among graduates amid a slowdown in the tech sector.

articles.headline: IT Hardware PLI Scheme Revamp Likely This Year for AI Servers
articles.summary: The government may modify the Rs 17,000 crore IT hardware PLI scheme in late FY27 to include AI servers and other AI-linked hardware, aiming to boost domestic manufacturing.

articles.headline: AI Reset Erases Nearly Half of Top IT Cos’ Value
articles.summary: India's top 10 software exporters may lose $200 billion in market value from their peak due to AI's disruptive impact, with some stocks down 30-50%.
